title: Are earthquake swarms along the Cotabato Trench echoes of a 1976 magnitude 8.1 quake?
content: Scientists explore recent earthquake swarms off the shores of Mindanao, a southern Philippine island, and consider similarities to the devastating 1976 Moro Gulf earthquake.
snippet: Scientists explore recent earthquake swarms off the shores of Mindanao, a southern Philippine island, and consider similarities to the devastating 1976 Moro Gulf earthquake.

title: Early warning systems are key to minimizing disasters
content: A study of one of the world’s longest-running disaster warning systems—desert locust monitoring—finds surveillance limits damages and generates returns of up to 680 times the investment.
snippet: A study of one of the world’s longest-running disaster warning systems—desert locust monitoring—finds surveillance limits damages and generates returns of up to 680 times the investment.

title: When drought did not win: Disaster Risk Financing Ignites Hope in Mozambique’s Magude province
content: As of October 2025, 6,728 people in Magude have benefited from improved water access, including 2,561 women. Approximately 8,797 cattle have benefited from improved water access, and 1,045 households received food assistance during drought periods.
snippet: As of October 2025, 6,728 people in Magude have benefited from improved water access, including 2,561 women. Approximately 8,797 cattle have benefited from improved water access, and 1,045 households

title: Programmatic responses to extreme heat - guidance for humanitarian practitioners
content: A comprehensive, sector-by-sector practical guide helping humanitarian organizations understand, prepare for, and respond to the rapidly growing threat of extreme heat and heatwaves.
snippet: A comprehensive, sector-by-sector practical guide helping humanitarian organizations understand, prepare for, and respond to the rapidly growing threat of extreme heat and heatwaves.

title: Passive cooling for public buildings - guidance for humanitarian practitioners
content: A practical, non-technical guide helping humanitarian workers keep health centres and schools cooler through low-cost, energy-free building strategies suited to hot climates.
snippet: A practical, non-technical guide helping humanitarian workers keep health centres and schools cooler through low-cost, energy-free building strategies suited to hot climates.

title: Women's well-being and everyday adaptation to heat
content: This review examines how extreme heat affects women’s well-being and highlights everyday adaptation practices across Africa, Asia and Oceania.
snippet: This review examines how extreme heat affects women’s well-being and highlights everyday adaptation practices across Africa, Asia and Oceania.

title: New ICT toolkit for integrated climate change assessment developed by EU-funded NEVERMORE project
content: The EU-funded NEVERMORE project has developed an ICT toolkit, which combines climate science, socio-economic data, and stakeholder knowledge to promote integrated climate change assessment.
snippet: The EU-funded NEVERMORE project has developed an ICT toolkit, which combines climate science, socio-economic data, and stakeholder knowledge to promote integrated climate change assessment.

title: Wildfire risk is now spreading to cool climates like the Scottish Highlands and Irish uplands
content: The most destructive wildfire season on record in Europe was in 2025, with more than one million hectares burned and tens of thousands of people displaced by fires across the continent.
snippet: The most destructive wildfire season on record in Europe was in 2025, with more than one million hectares burned and tens of thousands of people displaced by fires across the continent.

title: The increasing risk of heatwaves to vulnerable populations in eastern Australia
content: This study investigates historical trends in heatwave characteristics across New South Wales (NSW), identifies regions with increasing exposure in recent decades, and assesses risks to vulnerable populations using a combined heatwave risk framework.
snippet: This study investigates historical trends in heatwave characteristics across New South Wales (NSW), identifies regions with increasing exposure in recent decades, and assesses risks to vulnerable popu

title: In Bihar, groundwater treatment units were installed in regions that didn’t need them
content: Arsenic-contaminated groundwater affects more than 230 million people living in 108 countries. About 180 million of these people live in the Indian subcontinent (which includes Bangladesh, Nepal, and Pakistan, in addition to India) and Southeast Asia.
snippet: Arsenic-contaminated groundwater affects more than 230 million people living in 108 countries. About 180 million of these people live in the Indian subcontinent (which includes Bangladesh, Nepal, and

title: Something coming: what scientists know about a potential 'super' El Nino
content: Forecasters say a potentially "super" El Niño is rapidly taking shape in the Pacific—but whether it evolves into a history-making event could hinge on fickle winds and other volatile atmospheric shifts.
snippet: Forecasters say a potentially "super" El Niño is rapidly taking shape in the Pacific—but whether it evolves into a history-making event could hinge on fickle winds and other volatile atmospheric shift

title: Mozambique launches digital platform to improve irrigation monitoring
content: Designed to assess and diagnose irrigation systems, the platform provides water managers actionable data to boost performance and agriculture sustainability.
snippet: Designed to assess and diagnose irrigation systems, the platform provides water managers actionable data to boost performance and agriculture sustainability.

title: Size isn’t everything – volcanic eruptions may cause more global disruption than previously thought
content: Earthquakes have repeatedly shaken Egypt, including the magnitude 5.8 Cairo earthquake in 1992, which dislodged some of the pyramid’s outer casing stones. Yet the Great Pyramid remained essentially intact.
snippet: Earthquakes have repeatedly shaken Egypt, including the magnitude 5.8 Cairo earthquake in 1992, which dislodged some of the pyramid’s outer casing stones. Yet the Great Pyramid remained essentially in

title: How the Great Pyramid of Giza has survived 4,500 years of Egyptian earthquakes
content: Earthquakes have repeatedly shaken Egypt, including the magnitude 5.8 Cairo earthquake in 1992, which dislodged some of the pyramid’s outer casing stones. Yet the Great Pyramid remained essentially intact.

title: On Southeast Asia’s largest lake, locals wield tech to defend the flooded forest
content: Tonle Sap in Cambodia is the largest lake in Southeast Asia. Each year, when the dry season sets in, the waters of the flooded forest recede, the mangrove roots poke out through the mud, and the flooded forest turns into a tinder box.
snippet: Tonle Sap in Cambodia is the largest lake in Southeast Asia. Each year, when the dry season sets in, the waters of the flooded forest recede, the mangrove roots poke out through the mud, and the flood

title: Prescribed burns and forest thinning averted millions of tons of emissions and billions in damage
content: In addition to preventing an estimated 2.7 million tons of carbon emissions and $2.8 billion in damages, UC Davis researchers determined that fuel treatments prevented nearly 60 premature deaths.
snippet: In addition to preventing an estimated 2.7 million tons of carbon emissions and $2.8 billion in damages, UC Davis researchers determined that fuel treatments prevented nearly 60 premature deaths.

title: Addressing Southern Africa climate shocks: advocating for child-centred climate resilience and fiscal prioritization
content: The report addresses the escalating climate crisis in Southern Africa, where the region has swung from severe El Niño-driven drought in 2023-2024 to devastating La Niña floods and cyclones in 2025-2026, affecting nearly 1.9 million people.
snippet: The report addresses the escalating climate crisis in Southern Africa, where the region has swung from severe El Niño-driven drought in 2023-2024 to devastating La Niña floods and cyclones in 2025-202

title: Psychosocial adaptation as a coping strategy among older farmers facing drought in Thailand
content: The study examines how older farmers (aged 60–69) in drought-prone areas of Buriram Province, Thailand, perceive and respond to drought risk.
snippet: The study examines how older farmers (aged 60–69) in drought-prone areas of Buriram Province, Thailand, perceive and respond to drought risk.

title: A world on the edge: priorities for a pandemic‑resilient future
content: The 2026 GPMB report finds that as infectious disease outbreaks become more frequent they are also becoming more damaging, with widening health, economic, political and social impacts, and less capacity to recover from them.
snippet: The 2026 GPMB report finds that as infectious disease outbreaks become more frequent they are also becoming more damaging, with widening health, economic, political and social impacts, and less capaci

title: Managing disaster risks in electoral processes
content: In 2024 alone, extreme weather disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries and this is only the beginning. Evidence indicates that natural hazards are increasingly becoming a threat to elections, especially in regions vulnerable to extreme weather events.
snippet: In 2024 alone, extreme weather disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries and this is only the beginning. Evidence indicates that natural hazards are increasingly becoming a threat to elections, especiall

title: Towards multimodal geospatial reasoning: a foundation model approach for disaster detection from social media, news, and weather data
content: This publication explores how generative language models combine social media, news and weather data to detect disasters quickly and accurately, using satellite data to validate results from flood and wildfire case studies.
snippet: This publication explores how generative language models combine social media, news and weather data to detect disasters quickly and accurately, using satellite data to validate results from flood and

title: Bridging warning and adaptation addressing risk communication strategies for short-term natural hazard warnings and long-term risk adaptation – A scoping review
content: This review analyzes peer-reviewed studies to examine how risk communication strategies vary across temporal dimensions, hazard groups and intended purpose.
snippet: This review analyzes peer-reviewed studies to examine how risk communication strategies vary across temporal dimensions, hazard groups and intended purpose.

title: The next era of Atlantic hurricanes could be far more destructive
content: Recent research suggests climate change could supercharge swings between quiet years and hyperactive seasons packed with dangerous storms.
snippet: Recent research suggests climate change could supercharge swings between quiet years and hyperactive seasons packed with dangerous storms.

title: Quantifying uncertainty in climate change impacts: A Bayesian approach for the Cagayan river basin
content: This study develops a probabilistic flood-impact assessment for the Cagayan river basin (northern Luzon, Philippines) by linking Bayesian extreme-value inference with uncertainty propagation along the rainfall–flood–damage chain.
snippet: This study develops a probabilistic flood-impact assessment for the Cagayan river basin (northern Luzon, Philippines) by linking Bayesian extreme-value inference with uncertainty propagation along the

title: As floods get worse, Britain tries a new solution: beavers
content: Scientists have enlisted some of the animal kingdom's best flood engineers — beavers — to help.
snippet: Scientists have enlisted some of the animal kingdom's best flood engineers — beavers — to help.

title: African cities use nature to fight floods and climate change
content: From Kinshasa to Dire Dawa, cities across Africa are discovering that wetlands, trees and parks could be their strongest defense against climate change.
snippet: From Kinshasa to Dire Dawa, cities across Africa are discovering that wetlands, trees and parks could be their strongest defense against climate change.

title: The fiery tornadoes that could mop up oil spills
content: More than 15 years after the Deepwater Horizon disaster, researchers are still searching for new ways to tackle disastrous spills. Some are looking to flaming twisters.
snippet: More than 15 years after the Deepwater Horizon disaster, researchers are still searching for new ways to tackle disastrous spills. Some are looking to flaming twisters.

title: ‘Preventive engineering’ urged to reduce summer storm damage
content: Loans to help owners strengthen houses a better use of money than compensation after the fact.
snippet: Loans to help owners strengthen houses a better use of money than compensation after the fact.

title: Ready of not? Hurricane preparedness, response, and recovery of farms, forests, and rural communities in the U.S. Caribbean
content: This study highlights that forest owners, and rural communities in the Caribbean are among the groups most vulnerable to hurricanes and other extreme climate events.
snippet: This study highlights that forest owners, and rural communities in the Caribbean are among the groups most vulnerable to hurricanes and other extreme climate events.

title: Community risk assessment of flooding and heat hazards available for Baltimore
content: Chesapeake communities are at a heightened and increasing risk for flooding and coastal hazards.
snippet: Chesapeake communities are at a heightened and increasing risk for flooding and coastal hazards.

title: The hantavirus outbreak is the warning the world needs to improve pandemic preparedness
content: The latest case of a Canadian passenger testing positive shows the hantavirus outbreak isn’t over yet. We can probably expect more cases, given the long incubation period of this infection.
snippet: The latest case of a Canadian passenger testing positive shows the hantavirus outbreak isn’t over yet. We can probably expect more cases, given the long incubation period of this infection.

title: Radiotherapy continuity for cancer treatment: Lessons learned from natural disasters
content: This study examines the impact of natural disasters on the continuity of radiotherapy services, a critical component of cancer treatment responsible for managing one of the world's leading causes of death.
snippet: This study examines the impact of natural disasters on the continuity of radiotherapy services, a critical component of cancer treatment responsible for managing one of the world's leading causes of d

title: Characterizing changes in postfire debris-flow hazard as burned areas recover
content: Updating post-fire debris-flow hazard thresholds with satellite-tracked vegetation recovery reduces false alarms and improves predictions for years 2 and 3 after a wildfire.
snippet: Updating post-fire debris-flow hazard thresholds with satellite-tracked vegetation recovery reduces false alarms and improves predictions for years 2 and 3 after a wildfire.

title: Are the poor more exposed to climate hazards in Latin America?
content: This paper addresses two main questions. First, what proportion of people are exposed to climate hazards in Latin America and the Caribbean, especially among the poor versus the nonpoor?
snippet: This paper addresses two main questions. First, what proportion of people are exposed to climate hazards in Latin America and the Caribbean, especially among the poor versus the nonpoor?

title: Women experience extreme heat differently to men. And they’re adapting to it in creative ways
content: This research review, which looks at examples across Oceania, Africa and Asia finds that the people who are forced to adapt to the greatest extent are the ones that climate policies ignore the most.
snippet: This research review, which looks at examples across Oceania, Africa and Asia finds that the people who are forced to adapt to the greatest extent are the ones that climate policies ignore the most.

title: Disaster risk financing: Annual report 2025
content: The report provides deep dives into the DRF programmes in each country, as well as stories from the field, interviews with partners and insights into how WFP promotes the use of pre-arranged financing.
snippet: The report provides deep dives into the DRF programmes in each country, as well as stories from the field, interviews with partners and insights into how WFP promotes the use of pre-arranged financing

title: A swarm of earthquakes in South Africa’s Karoo Basin poses questions for oil and gas development
content: A recent study cautions that the Karoo, a potential target for shale gas exploration, might not be as seismologically calm as it appears.
snippet: A recent study cautions that the Karoo, a potential target for shale gas exploration, might not be as seismologically calm as it appears.

title: Sea level rise is accelerating, scientists confirm
content: Human-driven climate change is driving the rise of sea levels, worsening flood conditions and threatening coastal communities around the world. Not only is sea level rising, but it’s rising faster every year.
snippet: Human-driven climate change is driving the rise of sea levels, worsening flood conditions and threatening coastal communities around the world. Not only is sea level rising, but it’s rising faster eve

title: Vanuatu Marks First International Day in Memory of the Victims of Earthquakes
content: On 29 April 2026, Vanuatu marked its first national observance of the International Day in Memory of the Victims of Earthquakes, bringing together partners to strengthen earthquake preparedness and disaster resilience.
snippet: On 29 April 2026, Vanuatu marked its first national observance of the International Day in Memory of the Victims of Earthquakes, bringing together partners to strengthen earthquake preparedness and di

title: Disability-inclusive disaster risk reduction in Amadora: building resilience through local action
content: Building resilience for people with disabilities requires ensuring that their needs are fully recognised and integrated into disaster risk reduction (DRR) planning.
snippet: Building resilience for people with disabilities requires ensuring that their needs are fully recognised and integrated into disaster risk reduction (DRR) planning.
